market_data_input.key_trends: Secteur leader: energy (+37.6% YTD) | Région leader: south-korea (+31.4% YTD) | 5 secteur(s) en refroidissement (3M ≤ -2.0%) | 52W leader: energy (+32.5% sur 12 mois) | 6M leader: energy (+33.8% sur 6 mois)
market_data_input.risks: Secteurs en difficulté: financials, communication-services, information-technology | Régions en difficulté: germany, switzerland, italy | Concentration: peu de secteurs en zone favorable (rotation possible) | 5 secteurs avec 6M négatif (momentum moyen-terme dégradé)
market_data_input.sector_momentum_summary: materials(neutral,b=0.85,d=0.03%), energy(favored,b=0.74,d=1.76%), financials(avoided,b=0.94,d=-1.63%), communication-services(avoided,b=1.12,d=-2.51%), information-technology(avoided,b=1.33,d=-1.22%), consumer-staples(neutral,b=0.25,d=1.34%), industrials(neutral,b=0.9,d=-0.61%), utilities(neutral,b=0.37,d=-0.81%), real-estate(neutral,b=0.52,d=-0.22%), healthcare(avoided,b=0.55,d=-1.19%), consumer-discretionary(avoided,b=1.1,d=-2.14%)

ai_response.regime_rationale: Brent near $98 (5d avg >$101) drives persistent energy inflation with PCE at 2.8% well above target, while Fed is on hold (0 cuts in 6m) and multiple high-beta sectors (tech, financials, comm services, consumer discretionary) are in retreat — classic stagflationary mix of sticky inflation + softening growth. VIX at 22 falling and HY spreads tightening prevent escalation to risk_off, but the breadth deterioration (5 sectors cooling, only energy leading) signals fragile growth underneath.

adjustments.action: Increase energy/oil cap — dominant sector with macro tailwind
adjustments.rationale: Energy is +37.6% YTD, sole favored sector, and Brent near $100 sustains cash flows; stagflation historically rewards commodity producers.

adjustments.action: Reduce AI/tech cap — high beta, negative momentum, rate-sensitive
adjustments.rationale: IT sector avoided by RADAR (-1.22% daily), Fed on hold with PCE 2.8% means no rate relief for long-duration growth; second-order: sticky inflation → higher-for-longer → multiple compression.

adjustments.action: Reduce semi cap — cyclical + tech overlap in stagflation
adjustments.rationale: Semiconductors are high-beta tech with cyclical demand exposure; both growth slowdown and rate headwinds work against them in stagflation.

adjustments.action: Increase gold hedge — inflation protection + geopolitical premium
adjustments.rationale: Gold at $4739 despite 11% drawdown from ATH signals strong structural bid; stagflation is gold's best regime — real rates compressed by inflation while growth falters, plus Brent near $100 implies geopolitical risk premium.

adjustments.action: Increase dividend/value cap — defensive quality in stagflation
adjustments.rationale: Consumer staples (+1.34% daily) outperforming broad market; dividend/value provides inflation pass-through and lower duration equity exposure in stagflation.

adjustments.action: Prefer TIPS + shorten duration — inflation protection with rate risk management
adjustments.rationale: PCE 2.8% + 5Y breakeven 2.56% means TIPS still fairly priced for upside inflation surprise from energy; short duration protects against higher-for-longer Fed.

adjustments.action: Raise bond floor for Stable — increase defensive allocation
adjustments.rationale: Stable at 37.9% bonds is light for a stagflationary regime where equity breadth is deteriorating; adding short TIPS/treasury improves risk-adjusted returns.

adjustments.action: Increase precious metals cap — silver/gold miners benefit from inflation + gold strength
adjustments.rationale: Silver at $75 confirms broad precious metals bid; miners offer leveraged exposure to gold in stagflation with operational leverage to rising metal prices.

bond_strategy.regime_summary: Short-duration TIPS-heavy strategy: protect against sticky inflation while limiting rate risk from a Fed that cannot cut with PCE at 2.8%.

cash_allocation.rationale: Moderate cash as dry powder: VIX 22 falling suggests no imminent crash but stagflation regimes are prone to sudden risk-off episodes; cash provides optionality to buy dips in energy/gold if geopolitical escalation causes temporary selloff.

ai_response._cash_guidance: MINIMUM CASH: Agressif 2% if VIX>20 (option d'achat sur la vol), Modéré 3%, Stable 0% (T-bills = cash productif). Le cash est une POSITION, pas une absence de position.

crypto_allocation.rationale: Crypto is a risk asset that underperforms in stagflation; reduced caps but not zero for Agressif as BTC has partial digital-gold narrative that may hold with gold at $4700+.

warnings: OIL SUPPLY SHOCK ESCALATION: Brent 5d avg >$101 — if sustained above $105, CPI will re-accelerate forcing Fed to consider hikes, shifting regime toward crisis; monitor weekly EIA inventories and OPEC+ rhetoric.
BREADTH COLLAPSE: Only 1 of 11 sectors favored (energy) with 5 cooling — historically this concentration precedes sharp corrections when the leading sector rolls over.

FED POLICY ERROR RISK: Fed on hold at 3.64% with PCE 2.8% and 2s10s at +50bps — if they cut prematurely (market pricing), inflation re-anchors higher; if they hike, recession risk spikes. Binary outcome.

DOLLAR WEAKNESS ACCELERATING: DTWEXBGS at 120.7 (neutral-to-weak zone) with EUR/USD at 1.17 — further dollar weakness amplifies imported inflation via energy prices, creating a negative feedback loop.
GOLD DRAWDOWN 11% FROM ATH: Despite bullish structural thesis, a further correction to -15% is possible on any risk-off deleveraging; size positions for volatility.
S&P +3.8% DAILY MOVE: Single-day moves >3% in either direction are characteristic of unstable regimes — this could be a bear market rally or short squeeze rather than genuine risk-on; do not chase.
